made_changes: Changed files: support/operator/label_map.json, support/dashboard/src/data/labels.js, support/operator/reporter.py, support/operator/report_sinks.py, support/operator/run.py, support/operator/walker_kernel.py, support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py, support/checkers/check_package_path_admission.py, support/checkers/profiles/read_side_projection_boundary.yaml.
Added canonical Korean label data and dashboard parity pin.
Slack text now uses customer-language headline fields: building, status, work stage, role, action, one compact operator refs line, and proof-limit line.
Reporter packets now carry current_work_kind and current_lane projected from declared plan/building-map evidence.
run.py and walker_kernel.py now emit best-effort render-to-deliver notifications after evidence writes; absent Slack/dashboard env keeps delivery to local inbox only.
Checker pins now cover label parity, Slack message shape, auto-wiring, fake Slack sender invocation, local-inbox write, and no scheduler/thread/queue constructs.
not_proven: Not source truth, not success judgment, not quality judgment, not Movement authority.
Real Slack API delivery, real dashboard delivery, and reader-noticed notification behavior are not proven; fake sender and env-gating were proven.
run_building_once notification behavior was wired but not separately temp-driven by a dedicated behavioral checker.
Default auto-wire proof covers terminal/intervention events; building_started remains policy-admitted, not proven as a default event.

AGENTS.md referenced archive/status-kernel/current-brick-operating-packet-0531.md and archive/status-kernel/brick-protocol-post-d-surface-recompile-0-plan-0530.md, but those files were not present in this checkout.
Future Building correctness, provider quality, repeated provider reliability, and complete absence of future support-authority leaks remain not_proven.
